fault_hypothesis: Lines 67-68 incorrectly remove the last element when both inputs share the same last value, but a correct merge should keep all duplicates. The pop() logic is entirely wrong and should not exist.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the merged list length must still equal the sum of both input lengths — this is violated when the pop incorrectly removes a duplicate.
After executing the then-branch (the pop), the merged list length must equal the total input length.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(merged__1) > len(left) + len(right)

constraints.intent: The condition triggering the pop should never be true for a correctly merged list of the right length — if it fires, it indicates a bug.
The returned merged list must always have length equal to the sum of both input lengths.
